summary: Completed comprehensive backend testing of NASSAQ Scheduling Engine APIs - Iteration 7. All 29 tests passed (100% success rate). Tested: Time Slots CRUD, Teacher Assignments CRUD, School Schedules CRUD, Schedule Sessions CRUD, Auto-generate Schedule, Conflict Detection, Teacher Rank Updates, Teacher Workload Calculation.
passed_tests: GET /api/time-slots - Returns time slots for school (11 slots found)
POST /api/time-slots - Creates new time slot successfully
POST /api/teacher-assignments - Creates new assignment (or returns 400 if exists)
POST /api/schedules - Creates new schedule with draft status
GET /api/schedules/{id}/conflicts - Detects teacher and class double bookings
GET /api/teachers/{id}/workload - Returns workload with limits by rank
Unauthorized access - Returns 403 without auth token
critical_code_review_comments: Conflict detection is working correctly - found 2 conflicts in test schedule (teacher and class double booking)
Teacher workload calculation correctly identifies underloaded teachers based on rank limits
All CRUD operations properly handle soft deletes (is_active=False, status=cancelled/archived)
seed_data_creation: Time slots already seeded for test school (11 slots including breaks)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All scheduling engine APIs are working correctly. Test school (d3addce7-919b-4f5a-ba0c-0c2dc71599e9) has: 11 time slots, 1 teacher assignment, 1 schedule with 9 sessions, 2 teachers, 20 classes, 9 subjects. Conflict detection found 2 conflicts in existing schedule (teacher and class double booking on Tuesday). Teacher workload API correctly calculates limits based on rank (expert: 12-18, advanced: 16-20, practitioner: 18-24, assistant: 20-26 weekly hours).
